source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Geist for English product UI with Noto Sans JP guidance for Japanese headings, body text, labels, and numeric dashboard rhythm.
layout: Dense 8px product grids, ribbed kelp line dividers, cold-ocean panels, amber signal states, and water-blue data surfaces for immersive operational dashboards.

prompts.label: Transparent kelp canopy motif source
prompts.workflow: Built-in image_gen generation on a flat #ff00ff chroma-key background, copied into the workspace, then converted to RGBA with local chroma-key removal.
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif cutout source for public/systems/morphous-kelp-forest/motif.png
Primary request: Create only a kelp forest canopy motif, isolated full subject, no environment, no props, no text.
Subject: A sculptural cluster of cold-ocean kelp canopy fronds with long ribbed olive blades, amber golden edges, flexible stipes, small air bladders, layered translucent leaf texture, and subtle water-blue shadow detail. The subject should feel botanical and marine, suitable as a premium design-system motif.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; show the canopy crown and flowing blades without cropping any frond tips.
Lighting/mood: clean studio light that preserves olive kelp, amber kelp, blue-water depth shadows, satin wet texture, and fine blade veining.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no text, no watermark, no ocean scene, no rocks, no fish, no bubbles, no decorative background, no second subject, no green-screen color in the subject.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board for /systems/morphous-kelp-forest/design-system-light.png
Primary request: Generate a comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Kelp Forest" derived from the visible transparent kelp canopy cutout.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from the kelp motif: olive kelp canopy, amber kelp highlights, water blue depth shadows, cold-ocean mist neutrals, ribbed frond texture, flowing canopy arcs, and immersive data-system mood. The board is the implementation reference for coding a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible theme, so token roles and component examples must be clear and readable.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 4K-class composition.
Resolution and legibility: make the board feel high resolution and readable after gallery downscaling. Use large section labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections with exactly these short labels: Morphous Kelp Forest, Kelp Forest, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows, Assets.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; use labels like Geist, Noto Sans JP, Heading, Body, Label, Metrics. Show multilingual product UI guidance with short readable labels only.
UI coverage: buttons, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command menu, sidebar navigation, top navigation, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, map/depth tile, empty state, loading state, error state, spacing, radius, border, shadow, texture samples.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, realistic UI mockup, precise grid, dense but organized data interface, 8px radii, cold-ocean operational dashboard, kelp rib line dividers, translucent water panels, amber signal accents, olive canopy primary actions.
Color palette: use only motif-derived colors plus near-neutrals from the motif: kelp mist, cold foam, canopy olive, amber kelp, water blue, deep current, blue-black trench, sediment gray, pale gold highlight.
Text: use short readable UI labels; no paragraphs.
Constraints: no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple gradients, no tropical palette, no fish or animals, no watermark, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Kelp Forest".

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board for /systems/morphous-kelp-forest/design-system-dark.png
Primary request: Generate a dark-mode version of the visible Morphous Kelp Forest light design-system board. It must feel like the same system translated to dark mode, not a new theme.
Reference use: Use the visible transparent kelp canopy cutout and visible light-mode Morphous Kelp Forest board as references. Preserve the olive kelp canopy primary, amber kelp signal accents, water blue data accents, cold-ocean neutrals, ribbed frond texture, flowing canopy arcs, Japanese typography, and shadcn/tweakcn component coverage. The board is the implementation reference for dark-mode tokens and component states.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 4K-class composition.
Resolution and legibility: make the board feel high resolution and readable after gallery downscaling. Use large section labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections with exactly these short labels: Morphous Kelp Forest, Kelp Forest, Dark Mode, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows, Assets.
Visual details: deep current and blue-black trench background, cold foam text, panels in dark olive-blue and slate water neutrals, primary actions in canopy olive, amber kelp used sparingly for warning and data signal, water blue rings and charts, soft caustic texture overlays, ribbed kelp line dividers, 8px radii, crisp operational analytics layout.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; use labels like Geist, Noto Sans JP, Heading, Body, Label, Metrics.
UI coverage: buttons, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, command menu, sidebar navigation, top navigation, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, map/depth tile, empty/loading/error states, spacing, radius, border, shadow, texture samples.
Text: short readable UI labels only; no paragraphs.
Constraints: no neon overload, no unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple gradients, no tropical palette, no flat pure-black-only background, no fish or animals, no watermark, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Kelp Forest".
